title: Ask a Clarifying Question
summary: Ask a question to clear up confusion without feeling awkward.
learnerPromise: You will be able to ask questions that clear up confusion so you can act with confidence.
whyItMatters: Misunderstandings waste time and cause mistakes. Asking a quick clarifying question shows you are engaged and helps everyone stay on the same page.
realLifeUses: Asking a teacher to explain part of an assignment you did not understand
Confirming instructions from a manager before starting a task
Checking details with a friend about where and when to meet
Asking a doctor to repeat or rephrase medical advice
result: A clear question asked that resolves confusion about something you heard or read
youWillLearn: Recognize when you are confused before acting
Phrase a question that is specific and easy to answer
Ask without apologizing for not knowing
Repeat back what you heard to confirm you got it right
miniChallenge: Think of something you heard recently that you were not fully sure about. Write down and then ask one specific question that would clear it up.
steps: Notice that you are unsure about something someone said or wrote
Identify the exact part you do not understand
Form a short, specific question about that part
Ask the question directly without over-apologizing
Listen to the answer and repeat it back in your own words to confirm
completionCriteria: You asked a specific question about something you were unsure of
You got an answer that cleared up the confusion
You confirmed your understanding by restating what you learned
commonProblems: Pretending to understand to avoid looking foolish
Asking a question that is too vague to get a useful answer
Over-apologizing, which can make the question feel bigger than it is
tips: Questions like 'When you said X, did you mean Y?' are very effective
Asking for clarification is a sign of good communication, not weakness
If you are in a group, others are probably wondering the same thing
